codeReview.summary: The commit adds recursive nested sensitive shapes and metadata exposure, but it does not add the promised compile-surface regression proving nested sensitive derives avoid unnecessary trait bounds. Metadata exposure and unsupported-wrapper compile-fail coverage are present.

issues.description: The trybuild suite does not include any fixture covering VAL-NS-008. There is no compile-pass case proving nested sensitive derives work when plaintext child types intentionally omit non-essential traits like PartialEq/Eq/Display, so the feature contract about slimming the generated Encrypted* trait burden is unproven.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill still tells workers to add or update failing tests first, but this feature was treated as verification-only because the implementation was already on HEAD. The transcript shows that behavior is currently justified and the skill may need explicit verification-only guidance for already-landed features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 8f8c286b-2f2f-494b-98bc-980326db7578 and handoff.skillFeedback suggestedChanges both note a no-code verification flow for already-implemented features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: GitNexus impact analysis was not performed because callable GitNexus tooling was unavailable, and the current mission/skill guidance relies on a fallback to source inspection plus diff review. This repo-level convention exists in AGENTS/CLAUDE but is not actionable inside worker tooling without clearer integration availability guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md requires gitnexus_impact before edits; the worker transcript and handoff used only source inspection plus git diff/cargo validation and explicitly noted the fallback.

summary: Review failed: metadata exposure and wrapper-boundary checks are present, but the feature contract promised compile-surface proof that nested sensitive derives no longer impose unnecessary trait bounds, and that proof is missing from the committed tests.
